source: CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
title: Ensure Secure Boot for Shielded GKE Nodes is Enabled

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
input_row.title: Ensure Secure Boot for Shielded GKE Nodes is Enabled
input_row.description: Enable Secure Boot for Shielded GKE Nodes to verify the digital signature of node boot components.
input_row.rationale: An attacker may seek to alter boot components to persist malware or root kits during system initialisation. Secure Boot helps ensure that the system only runs authentic software by verifying the digital signature of all boot components, and halting the boot process if signature verification fails. Impact: Secure Boot will not permit the use of third-party unsigned kernel modules.
input_row.audit: Using Google Cloud Console: 1. Go to Kubernetes Engine by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/kubernetes/list. 2. From the list of clusters, click on the name of the cluster under test. 3. Open the Details pane for each Node pool within the cluster, and ensure that Secure boot is set to Enabled under the Security heading. Using Command Line: To check if Secure Boot is enabled for the Node pools in the cluster, run the following command for each Node pool: gcloud container node-pools describe <node_pool_name> --cluster <cluster_name> --zone <compute_zone> --format json | jq .config.shieldedInstanceConfig This will return the value below, if Secure Boot is enabled: { "enableSecureBoot": true }
input_row.remediation: Once a Node pool is provisioned, it cannot be updated to enable Secure Boot. New Node pools must be created within the cluster with Secure Boot enabled. Using Google Cloud Console: 1. Go to Kubernetes Engine by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/kubernetes/list 2. From the list of clusters, click on the cluster requiring the update and click ADD NODE POOL. 3. Ensure that the Secure boot checkbox is checked under the Shielded options Heading. 4. Click SAVE. Workloads will need to be migrated from existing non-conforming Node pools to the newly created Node pool, then delete the non-conforming pools. Using Command Line: To create a Node pool within the cluster with Secure Boot enabled, run the following command: gcloud container node-pools create <node_pool_name> --cluster <cluster_name> --zone <compute_zone> --shielded-secure-boot Workloads will need to be migrated from existing non-conforming Node pools to the newly created Node pool, then delete the non-conforming pools. Default Value: By default, Secure Boot is disabled in GKE clusters. By default, Secure Boot is disabled when Shielded GKE Nodes is enabled. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/how-to/shielded-gke- nodes#secure_boot 2. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/how-to/hardening-your-cluster
input_row.impact: Secure Boot will not permit the use of third-party unsigned kernel modules.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

- This control is centered around verifying the Secure Boot setting on Shielded GKE Nodes, which is a standard configuration property of GCP services: Kubernetes Engine and Compute Engine.
- The audit instructions detail the use of the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud`) to describe Node pools in GKE clusters and check the `shieldedInstanceConfig` property. This confirms that the property can be queried via GCP APIs.
- The command `gcloud container node-pools describe` specifically returns configuration details that include `enableSecureBoot`, which directly ties the property to compliance criteria.
- Since Secure Boot is a configuration setting of a GKE Node pool, this aspect is exposed through the Google Cloud SDK and CLI, making it API-checkable.
- The references provided guide on how to manipulate the configurations, further supporting the automation method.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_gke_node_pool_secure_boot_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- **GCP CLI Command**: Use `gcloud container node-pools describe <node_pool_name> --cluster <cluster_name> --zone <compute_zone> --format json` to query each Node pool within the GKE cluster.
- **Configuration Property to Validate**: Examine the `shieldedInstanceConfig` in the JSON output, particularly checking for `enableSecureBoot: true`.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria**: 
  - **Pass**: The `enableSecureBoot` field is set to `true` for all Node pools in the GKE cluster.
  - **Fail**: Any Node pool in the GKE cluster has `enableSecureBoot` set to `false` or the field is absent.
- **GCP Resource**: GKE Node pools associated with clusters.
- The program would systematically enumerate all clusters and Node pools across the specified regions or project accounts to ensure comprehensive compliance coverage.

**SOURCE:** CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
